vulns.title: Linux Kernel Incorrect Resource Transfer Between Spheres Vulnerability
vulns.summary: Linux Kernel contains an incorrect resource transfer between spheres vulnerability that could allow for privilege escalation.
vulns.technicalSummary: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: crypto: algif_aead - Revert to operating out-of-place This mostly reverts commit 72548b093ee3 except for the copying of the associated data.
vulns.breakdown: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: crypto: algif_aead - Revert to operating out-of-place This mostly reverts commit 72548b093ee3 except for the copying of the associated data.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/portbuster1337/lpe-toolkit
cd lpe-toolkit
make
./<compiled-poc> <authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.

vulns.title: Local Root Privilege Escalation and Credential Disclosure in the Linux Kernel ptrace Path

vulns.title: Multi-architecture Linux privilege escalation toolkit with 18 pre-built and runtime-compilable exploits.
vulns.summary: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: rxrpc: fix oversized RESPONSE authenticator length check rxgk_verify_response() decodes auth_len from the packet and is supposed to verify that it fits in the...
vulns.technicalSummary: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: rxrpc: fix oversized RESPONSE authenticator length check rxgk_verify_response() decodes auth_len from the packet and is supposed to verify that it fits in the remaining bytes.
vulns.breakdown: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: rxrpc: fix oversized RESPONSE authenticator length check rxgk_verify_response() decodes auth_len from the packet and is supposed to verify that it fits in the remaining bytes.

vulns.title: Multi-architecture Linux privilege escalation toolkit with 18 pre-built and runtime-compilable exploits.
vulns.summary: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: xfrm: esp: avoid in-place decrypt on shared skb frags MSG_SPLICE_PAGES can attach pages from a pipe directly to an skb.

vulns.title: Multi-architecture Linux privilege escalation toolkit with 18 pre-built and runtime-compilable exploits.
vulns.summary: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: net: skbuff: preserve shared-frag marker during coalescing skb_try_coalesce() can attach paged frags from @from to @to.

vulns.title: Unauthenticated Privilege Escalation CVE-2026-9018: Easy Elements for Elementor
vulns.summary: The Easy Elements for Elementor – Addons & Website Templates plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in all versions up to, and including, 1.4.5 via the `easyel_handle_register()` function.
vulns.technicalSummary: The Easy Elements for Elementor – Addons & Website Templates plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in all versions up to, and including, 1.4.5 via the `easyel_handle_register()` function.
vulns.breakdown: The Easy Elements for Elementor – Addons & Website Templates plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in all versions up to, and including, 1.4.5 via the `easyel_handle_register()` function.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/xxconi/CVE-2026-9018
cd CVE-2026-9018
python3 <script>.py --target https://<authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.
